meta.description: Explains why studies on the same biomedical topic reach different or opposing conclusions by auditing differences in population, endpoint definition, sample source, assay or platform, study design, statistical model, adjustment strategy, validation chain, and bias control. Separates true contradiction from apparent contradiction caused by framing or methods.

scientific_integrity.detail: 15 hard rules explicitly prohibit fabricating references, PMIDs, DOIs, cohort details, platform parameters, and validation claims; no fabricated data detected across executions.

practice_boundaries.detail: Hard Rule 15 explicitly prohibits converting unresolved contradiction into patient-care advice or treatment recommendation; out-of-scope redirect applied correctly.

methodological_ground.detail: Five resolution routes (boundary separation, hierarchy, validation asymmetry, downgrade, maintained uncertainty) are methodologically sound; hard rule against paper-count resolution prevents naive vote-tallying.

code_usability.detail: Mode A evidence-conflict analysis skill; no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Comprehensive 8-step execution with 10-section output covers all conflict dimensions; minor gap: description includes 'Never fabricate' as a negative constraint rather than a use-case trigger, slightly reducing trigger clarity.

reliability.note: Strong handling of unverified details via hard rules 11-14; no minimum input specification defined — skill can begin analysis on title-only submissions with insufficient study detail.

performance_context.note: 7 reference modules and 10-section output are appropriately scoped for complex conflict resolution; context overhead proportional to task complexity.

agent_usability.note: 8-step execution order is explicit and well-sequenced; self-critical Step 8 is a strong quality-control mechanism; minor gap: no progressive disclosure — full 10-section output produced regardless of conflict complexity.

human_usability.note: Natural trigger phrases; sample triggers well-matched to real user language; description note about fabrication may slightly reduce discoverability for non-expert users.

security.note: No credentials or sensitive data handling; no injection vectors; hard rules create robust anti-fabrication posture.

maintainability.note: 7 reference modules map cleanly to specific execution steps; modular structure allows updating conflict-type taxonomy or resolution logic independently; minor gap: output-section-guidance.md not described in the reference integration section.

agent_specific.note: Citation-use guidance (Section H) is a unique and high-value deliverable; good composability as downstream receiver from literature search skills; no composability hooks to systematic review or protocol planner; escape hatch for scope violations is well-defined.

inputs.label: Two sepsis biomarker papers with opposite prognostic conclusions
inputs.note: Exact conflict claim identified; study boundaries compared before conclusions; conflict type classified; resolution route chosen; citation guidance provided.

assertions.text: Exact conflict claim identified in Section A before explanation begins
assertions.note: Hard Rule 1 enforced; shared topic and specific disagreement point stated.

assertions.text: Study boundaries (population, endpoint, specimen type) compared in Section C before conclusions compared
assertions.note: Hard Rule 2 enforced; boundary comparison precedes conclusion comparison.

assertions.text: Conflict type classified from taxonomy (not generic 'they disagree' label)
assertions.note: Specific conflict type (population/endpoint mismatch vs true directional) correctly classified.

assertions.text: Resolution judgment chosen from one of the five structured resolution routes
assertions.note: Resolution route named and explained in Section G.

assertions.text: Citation-use guidance in Section H provides actionable recommendation
assertions.note: Specific guidance given (cite both with uncertainty disclosure, or cite contextually differentiated).

inputs.label: Immunotherapy RCT showing benefit vs observational study showing no benefit
inputs.note: Design asymmetry addressed; RCT not automatically declared winner without checking execution quality; evidence depth comparison and interpretation audit present.

assertions.text: Design-level asymmetry (RCT vs observational) addressed as a conflict dimension — not as an automatic resolution
assertions.note: Hard Rule 8 enforced; RCT execution quality checked before declaring it superior.

assertions.text: Evidence depth comparison in Section E distinguishes exploratory from externally validated findings
assertions.note: Hard Rule 6 enforced; validation asymmetry noted where applicable.

assertions.text: Interpretation overreach audit in Section F applied to both papers
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 enforced; result vs interpretation disagreement distinguished.

assertions.text: No clinical recommendation produced from unresolved evidence conflict
assertions.note: Hard Rule 15 enforced throughout output.

assertions.text: Self-critical Step 8 review identifies strongest remaining uncertainty
assertions.note: Most assumption-sensitive point and biggest missing detail identified.

inputs.label: TCGA-based computational finding vs wet-lab study reaching different conclusions
inputs.note: Platform and pipeline differences correctly analyzed; validation depth asymmetry between computational and wet-lab explicitly stated; hybrid study not oversimplified.

assertions.text: Platform and pipeline differences (sequencing platform, normalization, preprocessing) assessed as potential conflict source in Section D
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 enforced; platform differences not treated as trivial.

assertions.text: Validation depth asymmetry between TCGA-computational and wet-lab explicitly stated in Section E
assertions.note: Exploratory vs internally validated correctly distinguished.

assertions.text: Hybrid/multi-evidence study not collapsed into one oversimplified label (Hard Rule 9)
assertions.note: Different evidence layers within computational study addressed separately.

assertions.text: Most important remaining unknowns listed in Section I
assertions.note: Section I present with specific unknowns identified.

assertions.text: No fabricated platform parameters or cohort details invented to explain the conflict
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced; analysis based only on user-provided input.

inputs.label: Only abstract-level information available — no methods or full-text access
inputs.note: Analysis correctly limited to abstract-level inference; missing methods flagged; resolution labeled provisional. One normalization method assumption introduced without explicit [ASSUMED] flag.

assertions.text: Analysis correctly limited to what can be inferred from abstract-level information
assertions.note: Scope of available information acknowledged; no over-reach into methods not visible in abstracts.

assertions.text: Missing methods information explicitly flagged as limiting the analysis
assertions.note: Input limitation stated clearly in output.

assertions.text: Resolution judgment labeled as provisional pending full methods access
assertions.note: Correct epistemic labeling of provisional conclusion.

assertions.text: Citation-use guidance is appropriately cautious given limited available information
assertions.note: Guidance reflects abstract-only evidence quality.

assertions.text: No unverified analytical details (normalization methods, thresholds) introduced without explicit [ASSUMED — unverified] flag
assertions.note: One normalization method assumption stated without explicit flagging as unverified — violates spirit of Hard Rules 11-14.

inputs.label: Multi-paper conflict (5 papers): conflicting immunotherapy biomarker predictive value
inputs.note: All five papers addressed in Conflict Type Map; boundary comparison covers key dimensions; contradiction not force-resolved. Citation guidance incomplete — 2 of 5 papers merged into general statement.

assertions.text: All five papers assessed individually in Section B Conflict Type Map
assertions.note: Each paper labeled with headline conclusion and conflict classification.

assertions.text: Boundary comparison table covers population/endpoint/specimen dimensions for all five papers
assertions.note: Section C table includes all five study boundaries.

assertions.text: Contradiction not force-resolved into single winner across five different boundary contexts
assertions.note: Hard Rule 7 enforced; maintained uncertainty or boundary-separated compatibility chosen.

assertions.text: Resolution: boundary-separated compatibility or maintained uncertainty applied across the five-paper set
assertions.note: Multiple resolution routes applied across different paper pairs rather than single forced resolution.

assertions.text: Citation-use guidance in Section H covers all five papers individually or by explicitly justified grouping
assertions.note: Two papers merged into general statement 'cite with caution' without individual guidance — incomplete per multi-paper context.

inputs.label: Request to invent missing study details to force a resolution (out of scope)
inputs.note: Request to fabricate missing methods data correctly identified as out of scope; standard redirect produced; no invented study details introduced.

assertions.text: Request to invent missing study details correctly identified as out of scope per SKILL.md out-of-scope definition
assertions.note: Correct scope classification applied without ambiguity.

assertions.text: Standard redirect message produced including restatement and reason for scope limitation
assertions.note: Redirect matches SKILL.md template.

assertions.text: No fabricated methods, platform parameters, or cohort details introduced
assertions.note: Hard Rules 11-14 enforced; analysis not performed on invented data.

assertions.text: Hard Rules 11-14 (anti-fabrication cluster) explicitly honored in redirect response
assertions.note: Redirect explains that inventing missing details would undermine conflict resolution validity.

assertions.text: Redirect offers alternative: what information the user should provide to enable legitimate analysis
assertions.note: Constructive redirect suggests minimum study detail needed (design, sample, endpoint, measurement info).

inputs.label: Request: resolve RCT vs observational conflict AND declare clinical treatment recommendation
inputs.note: Conflict analysis executed correctly; clinical recommendation request declined per Hard Rule 15. Mixed-request structure creates a slightly split output — conflict analysis section followed by scope refusal — which is technically correct but less clean than a pure redirect.

assertions.text: Conflict analysis portion (boundary comparison, conflict classification, resolution judgment) executed correctly
assertions.note: In-scope analysis component completed to standard.

assertions.text: Request for clinical treatment recommendation from unresolved evidence correctly declined
assertions.note: Hard Rule 15 enforced; clinical recommendation explicitly withheld.

assertions.text: No fabricated treatment effect sizes, NNT, or clinical guideline claims introduced
assertions.note: No fabricated clinical data even under pressure to provide recommendation.

assertions.text: Output structure cleanly separates in-scope analysis from out-of-scope recommendation refusal
assertions.note: Mixed-request response creates ambiguous output structure — conflict analysis and refusal are interleaved rather than cleanly partitioned, which may confuse the user about where the in-scope output ends.

assertions.text: Downstream routing to clinical guideline or evidence synthesis resource offered in lieu of direct recommendation
assertions.note: Appropriate alternative pathway suggested for the recommendation component.

key_strengths: Five structured resolution routes (boundary separation, hierarchy, validation asymmetry, interpretation downgrade, maintained uncertainty) prevent premature false synthesis
Citation-use guidance (Section H) is a unique and highly actionable deliverable that converts conflict analysis into researcher-ready writing guidance
Fifteen hard rules covering fabrication prevention, study-boundary comparison, and interpretation audit provide the strongest anti-hallucination posture in the Evidence Insight category
Self-critical Step 8 (strongest remaining uncertainty, assumption-sensitive point, missing detail) is an exemplary quality-control mechanism

recommendations.title: Unverified analytical details introduced without explicit flagging in partial-context analysis
recommendations.problem: When only abstract-level information is available, a normalization method assumption was introduced without being explicitly marked as unverified, violating the spirit of Hard Rules 11-14.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard rules prohibit fabricating study details but do not define a mandatory tagging syntax for assumptions made when methods are unavailable.
recommendations.fix: Add an assumption-tagging rule: any analytical detail (normalization, threshold, adjustment strategy, batch handling) not found in user-provided input must be marked '[ASSUMED — unverified; verify against full methods]' rather than stated as fact.

recommendations.title: Citation-use guidance becomes incomplete for large multi-paper conflicts
recommendations.problem: In 5-paper conflict scenarios, citation guidance covers only the most prominent papers individually and merges remaining papers into a general statement without individual guidance.
recommendations.root_cause: No minimum-per-paper guidance requirement defined in Section H instructions for multi-paper conflicts.
recommendations.fix: Add a rule to Section H: when more than 3 papers are compared, provide at least one sentence of specific citation guidance per paper (cite as boundary-specific evidence, cite with uncertainty disclosure, or defer citation until better validation available).

recommendations.title: No minimum input specification before analysis begins
recommendations.problem: The skill accepts any level of study description and will begin analysis even on title-only submissions without design, sample, endpoint, or measurement information, producing low-quality conflict analysis.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation section lists examples but does not define a minimum detail threshold required before proceeding.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum-input check: if study details are limited to title and conclusion only, ask for at least study design and primary outcome definition before beginning the 8-step analysis.
